answers.5:
Render Phase: This is where the fiber nodes are built and diffing work goes on. This depends on the priority lanes and a scheduler takes care of running, pausing, prioritising and aborting execution. Thus is interruptible and asynchronous. 

Commit Phase: This i a synchronous task, once the commit phase starts it cannot be stopped. This starts when the WIP tree is flushed into Current Tree.
answers.6: Time slicing is a technique used in concurrent rendering to break down rendering work into smaller chunks, allowing the main thread to remain responsive to user interactions. This approach helps prevent the UI from freezing or becoming unresponsive during heavy rendering tasks.
answers.7:
There are 4 priority lanes:
Sync lane: Has the highest priority and needs immediate attention, any user interactions like click
Input continuous lane: When user drags or scrolls. Next to Sync lane
Transition lane: This is deferred lane for low priority tasks
Idle Lane: For the least when there is no other work.

It is intentional, and
is not directly related to concurrent rendering.
Strict Mode is a development tool that helps identify potential issues in an application by intentionally invoking 
certain lifecycle methods and effects more than once.
